We are asked to determine whether bortezomib is a proteasome inhibitor.
From the retrieved evidence, we find explicit confirmation in document [2]: "Bortezomib is a proteasome inhibitor." Furthermore, it details the exact mechanism — bortezomib reversibly binds to the chymotrypsin-like subunit of the 26S proteasome, thereby inhibiting its function and leading to the accumulation of pro-apoptotic proteins, ultimately triggering apoptosis in malignant cells. This biochemical explanation supports its classification as a proteasome inhibitor.
